name: Customer
description: A person or organisation that purchases products or services. This is the foundational contact record for tracking business relationships across acquisition, retention, and support workflows.

semantics.definition: The full name of the customer, used as the primary human-readable identifier in listings and communications.
The primary email address for the customer, used for communication, login identification, and deduplication.
The customer's phone number for direct contact, support escalation, or SMS communication.
The name of the organisation or business the customer is associated with, used for B2B segmentation and account grouping.
The current lifecycle status of the customer, indicating whether they are actively engaged, dormant, or have left.
The acquisition channel through which the customer was first obtained, used for attribution and marketing analysis.
The date the customer record was first created, marking the start of the business relationship.
Free-form notes about the customer, capturing context, preferences, or history that does not fit structured fields.

A customer represents a person or organisation that has a commercial relationship with the business. Customers are the central entity around which revenue, support, and engagement activities are organised.
